label: 284s Tick-borne diseases

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services (referred to in this section as “the Secretary”) shall continue to conduct or support epidemiological, basic, translational, and clinical research related to vector-borne diseases, including tick-borne diseases.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: b Reports
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that each triennial report under (as amended by section 2032) includes information on actions undertaken by the National Institutes of Health to carry out subsection (a) with respect to tick-borne diseases.section 283 of this title

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a working group, to be known as the Tick-Borne Disease Working Group (referred to in this section as the “Working Group”), comprised of representatives of appropriate Federal agencies and other non-Federal entities, to provide expertise and to review all efforts within the Department of Health and Human Services related to all tick-borne diseases, to help ensure interagency coordination and minimize overlap, and to examine research priorities.

children.label: i
children.text: ongoing tick-borne disease research, including research related to causes, prevention, treatment, surveillance, diagnosis, diagnostics, duration of illness, and intervention for individuals with tick-borne diseases;

children.label: ii
children.text: advances made pursuant to such research;

children.label: I
children.text: epidemiological activities related to tick-borne diseases; and

children.label: II
children.text: basic, clinical, and translational tick-borne disease research related to the pathogenesis, prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of tick-borne diseases;

children.label: iv
children.text: gaps in tick-borne disease research described in clause (iii)(II);

children.label: v
children.text: the Working Group’s meetings required under paragraph (4); and

children.label: vi
children.text: the comments received by the Working Group;

children.label: B
children.text: make recommendations to the Secretary regarding any appropriate changes or improvements to such activities and research; and

children.label: C
children.text: solicit input from States, localities, and nongovernmental entities, including organizations representing patients, health care providers, researchers, and industry regarding scientific advances, research questions, surveillance activities, and emerging strains in species of pathogenic organisms.

children.label: i
children.text: The Office of the Assistant Secretary for Health.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Food and Drug Administration.

children.label: iii
children.text: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

children.label: iv
children.text: The National Institutes of Health.

children.label: v
children.text: Such other agencies and offices of the Department of Health and Human Services as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: Physicians and other medical providers with experience in diagnosing and treating tick-borne diseases.

children.label: ii
children.text: Scientists or researchers with expertise.

children.label: iii
children.text: Patients and their family members.

children.label: iv
children.text: Nonprofit organizations that advocate for patients with respect to tick-borne diseases.

children.label: v
children.text: Other individuals whose expertise is determined by the Secretary to be beneficial to the functioning of the Working Group.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 4 Meetings
children.text: The Working Group shall meet not less than twice each year.

children.label: A
children.text: submit a report on its activities under paragraph (2)(A) and any recommendations under paragraph (2)(B) to the Secretary, the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: make such report publicly available on the Internet website of the Department of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: Applicability of FACA
children.label: 6 Applicability of FACA
children.text: The Working Group shall be treated as an advisory committee subject to the Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: 7 Sunset
children.text: The Working Group under this section shall terminate 6 years after .December 13, 2016
